SparSCit

SparSCit
  • API Reference
  • Embedding (sparscit.em)
  • View page source

Embedding (sparscit.em)

Dimensionality reduction and embedding methods for single-cell data. Includes PCA, spectral embedding, UMAP, dendrogram, and landscape embedding.

  • sparscit.embedding._pca.pca() — Principal Component Analysis

  • sparscit.embedding._spectral.spectral() — Spectral embedding for sparse data

  • sparscit.embedding._spectral.multiview_spectral() — Multi-view spectral embedding

  • sparscit.embedding._umap.umap() — UMAP embedding

  • sparscit.embedding._dendrogram.dendrogram() — Compute dendrogram

  • sparscit.embedding._landscape.landscape() — Create landscape embedding from neighborhood clusters

sparscit.embedding.dendrogram(adata: AnnData, y_key: str, *, random_seed: int = 0) → None[source]

Create embedding based on dendrogram order.

Writes a 2D embedding into adata.obsm['X_dendrogram'] where the x-coordinate is derived from community tree ordering and the y-coordinate from the specified observation key.

Parameters:

  • adata – Annotated data matrix with community tree information in adata.uns

  • y_key – Key in .obs to use for y-coordinate of cell on embedding

  • random_seed – Random seed for jitter applied to x-coordinates

Returns:

None – Writes the result into adata.obsm['X_dendrogram']

sparscit.embedding.landscape(adata: AnnData, scaffold: LandscapeScaffold, y_key: str, embedding_key: str, *, obsp_key: str | None = None, community_key: str = 'leiden', y_mean_init: bool = False, force_post_y: bool = True, umap_epochs: int = 5, umap_spread: float = 1, umap_min_dist: float = 0.05, return_coarse: bool = False) → None[source]

Create landscape based on neighborhood clusters, using leaf-clusters for inter-slice alignment. This function uses information from .obsp[‘distances’]

Parameters:

  • adata – AnnData object with neighbor graph in .obsp

  • scaffold – A LandscapeScaffold instance identifying leaf-clusters

  • y_key – Key in .obs to use for y-coordinate of cell on landscape. This is usually (pseudo)time

  • embedding_key – Embedding to use in UMAP transform

  • obsp_key – Key in .obsp for connectivity matrix. If None, uses ‘connectivities’

  • community_key – Categorical key in .obs for communities

  • y_mean_init – Whether to use y_key per cell y-coordinate (continuous) or per cluster-mean for initial embedding

  • force_post_y – Whether to replace y-coordinate post-UMAP with y_key or leave new y-coordinate generated by UMAP

  • umap_epochs – How many UMAP epochs to run

  • umap_spread – UMAP spread parameter

  • umap_min_dist – UMAP min_dist parameter

  • return_coarse – Whether to return coarse embedding

Returns:

  • adata.obsm[‘X_landscape’] – Landscape embedding

  • adata.obsm[‘X_coarse_landscape’] – Only if return_coarse is True

sparscit.embedding.multiview_spectral(adata: Any, layers: list[str], *, layer_weights: list[float] | None = None, n_components: int = 25, random_seed: int | None = None, use_all_features: bool = False, skip_idf: bool = False, weighted_by_sd: bool = True) → ndarray[source]

Multiview spectral embedding. Adapted from SnapATAC2, DOI: 10.1038/s41592-023-02139-9

Parameters:

  • adata – AnnData object with data in .layers

  • layers – List of layer keys to use for multi-view embedding

  • layer_weights – List of floats with layer importances. If None, equal weights are used

  • n_components – Number of components to generate

  • random_seed – Random seed for sampling data. If set to None, a seed is generated internally

  • use_all_features – Whether to use all features (ignoring .var[‘exclude’])

  • skip_idf – Skip IDF transformation. This is not recommended!

  • weighted_by_sd – Whether to weight eigenvectors by the square root of eigenvalues

Returns:

  • adata.obsm[‘X_multi_spectral’] – Embedding of dimensions (n, n_components)

  • eigenvalues – Eigenvalues that can be plotted with sparscit.pl.explained_variance_ratio()

sparscit.embedding.pca(adata: AnnData, embedding_key: str | None = None, *, layer_key: str | None = None, n_components: int = 30) → ndarray[Any, dtype[_ScalarType_co]][source]

PCA using sklearn.decomposition.PCA

Parameters:

  • adata – AnnData object

  • embedding_key – Key of embedding in .obsm to transform

  • layer_key – Alternatively, a layer can be transformed if this key is provided

  • n_components – Number of principal components to keep

Returns:

  • adata.obsm[‘X_pca’] – PCA embedding

  • variance_ratio – Variance ratio which can be plotted with sparscit.pl.explained_variance_ratio()

sparscit.embedding.spectral(adata: AnnData, layer: str, n_components: int = 25, use_all_features: bool = False, *, weighted_by_sd: bool = True) → ndarray[source]

Spectral embedding. Adapted from SnapATAC2, DOI: 10.1038/s41592-023-02139-9

Parameters:

  • adata – AnnData object with data in .layers

  • layer – Layer key for spectral embedding

  • n_components – Number of components to generate

  • use_all_features – Whether to use all features (ignoring .var[‘exclude’])

  • weighted_by_sd – Whether to weight eigenvectors by the square root of eigenvalues

Returns:

  • adata.obsm[‘X_spectral’] – Embedding of dimensions (n, n_components)

  • eigenvalues – Eigenvalues that can be plotted with sparscit.pl.explained_variance_ratio()

sparscit.embedding.umap(adata: AnnData, embedding_key: str, *, n_neighbors: int = 15, umap_kwargs: dict = {}) → None[source]

UMAP 2D embedding

Parameters:

  • adata – AnnData object with embedding in .obsm

  • embedding_key – Which embedding to transform in .obsm

  • n_neighbors – Number of neighbors to consider for calculating UMAP

  • umap_kwargs – Additional keyword arguments passed to umap.UMAP

Returns:

adata.obsm[“X_umap”] – UMAP embedding
